documents.title: University of Houston public AI posture sources

ai_strategy.summary: UH has a visible research and administrative AI posture, with major funded AI projects, a Bauer Human-Centered AI Institute, system AI guidance, and an IT strategy to create an AI Center of Excellence.

key_provisions: Use institutionally reviewed AI tools for sensitive university work.
Protect restricted, confidential, student, personnel, and research data when using AI tools.
Verify AI output and keep humans accountable for academic, administrative, and research decisions.
ai_policy.summary: University of Houston's public AI policy posture is comprehensive and emphasizes responsible use, privacy, and human accountability based on reviewed sources.

bodies.name: Bauer Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence Institute
bodies.scope: UH has a visible research and administrative AI posture, with major funded AI projects, a Bauer Human-Centered AI Institute, system AI guidance, and an IT strategy to create an AI Center of Excellence.

governance_structure.summary: Governance appears distributed across AI-facing offices, centers, technology units, teaching units, or committees; no standalone chief AI officer was confirmed unless named in leadership.

initiatives.title: Responsible AI guidance and governance
initiatives.description: University of Houston has public AI guidance, governance, or strategy resources that frame responsible adoption, privacy, academic integrity, and human oversight.
initiatives.significance: This is a concrete R1 signal of AI moving from isolated experimentation toward governed, mission-linked adoption.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in reviewed sources.

collaborations.description: Collaboration pattern inferred from the cited initiative scope when a named partner is not listed separately.

initiatives.policy_implications: Requires privacy, academic-integrity, and human-oversight controls as AI use scales.

initiatives.title: AI tools and campus enablement
initiatives.description: UH partnered with Google to provide Gemini for Education and NotebookLM to all students, faculty, and staff as a secure universitywide AI foundation. UH framed the rollout as part of an 'AI for All Coogs' commitment to ensure every student graduates AI-proficient, and reported more than 140 faculty researchers, over $70 million in AI-related funded research, four AI-focused centers or institutes, and 140+ AI courses across 14 colleges.
initiatives.significance: This is a campus-wide AI platform rollout with unusually specific public evidence for scope, student access, research funding, and curriculum breadth.

investment.description: No public dollar amount found in reviewed sources.

collaborations.description: Universitywide Gemini for Education and NotebookLM deployment.

research_centers.name: Bauer Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence Institute
research_centers.focus: student experience, industry collaboration, interdisciplinary AI research

computing_infrastructure.summary: UH's Carya supercomputer is a $2.5 million HPE cluster with 9,984 Intel CPU cores, 188 compute nodes, 20 GPU nodes, and 64 NVIDIA V100 32GB GPUs. It supports researchers across UH and the UH System through the HPE Data Science Institute and Research Computing Data Core.

investments_and_gifts.source: University of Houston / Governor's University Research Initiative / Hewlett Packard Enterprise
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Add a third HPE Data Science Institute supercomputing cluster for UH and UH System researchers.

investments_and_gifts.title: HPE Data Science Institute gift
investments_and_gifts.purpose: Launch and support the University of Houston Hewlett Packard Enterprise Data Science Institute and its high-performance computing services.

regulatory_context.summary: State/system context was checked for TX. Relevant public AI or system governance links are included where found.

narrative_summary: University of Houston is an R1 institution whose public AI posture is best understood as follows: UH has a visible research and administrative AI posture, with major funded AI projects, a Bauer Human-Centered AI Institute, system AI guidance, and an IT strategy to create an AI Center of Excellence. The evidence touches governance and policy, teaching and learning, research and innovation, enterprise enablement, student experience, workforce development, and equity or public-impact dimensions. The strongest public sources are first-party AI hubs, guidance pages, research center pages, academic-program materials, and state or system policy resources. Compared with the most centralized AI adopters, several details remain opaque, including named executive AI ownership, exact enterprise user counts, and total AI spending. Even so, the reviewed public record supports a medium-confidence assessment that AI is moving into curriculum, research infrastructure, technology governance, and student or workforce-facing programs rather than remaining purely ad hoc.

search_queries: University of Houston artificial intelligence strategy guidance
University of Houston generative AI policy faculty students
University of Houston AI research center program
University of Houston AI computing infrastructure partnership
University of Houston Gemini for Education NotebookLM all students faculty staff
University of Houston Carya supercomputer GPU count
gaps_acknowledged: No public chief AI officer was confirmed in reviewed sources.

UH discloses a $70 million-plus AI-related funded research portfolio, a $2.5 million Carya supercomputer, and a $10 million HPE Data Science Institute gift, but Gemini contract value and active-user metrics were not public.
Carya GPU capacity and Gemini/NotebookLM scope are public; no public campus-wide AI assessment redesign mandate was found.
No student subgroup outcome metrics were found beyond UH's broad AI for All Coogs access commitment.

strategic_archetype.rationale: Primary archetype selected from visible public signals: balanced enterprise.
